Here is the comprehensive summary of the project's current state, design changes, and configuration requirements. You can paste this into a new conversation to instantly onboard the AI or a new developer.
UBI TechSupport AI - Project Handoff Summary
1. Project Overview
A minimalist, high-end technical support dashboard for Ulticon Builders, Inc. The app uses Google Gemini 2.5 Flash to triage IT requests, enforce company protocols (PM-IT-04), and manage tickets via Google Sheets.
Live URL: http://13.229.219.21 (AWS Lightsail)
Stack: React 18, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Google GenAI SDK.
2. UI/UX Transformation (Current Design)
Full-Screen Chat: The application is no longer a 50/50 split. The AIChat component now takes up the entire screen width/height.
Floating Ticket Window: The TicketManager is now a "floating overlay" (modal style) positioned in the top-right.
Visibility Logic: It is hidden by default. It only appears when the AI successfully identifies a user (via PIN or Email) AND finds existing tickets for them.
Lock Screen Removed: The manual PIN entry form has been removed. Authentication happens naturally through the chat conversation.
3. New Functionalities & Workflows
File Uploads:
Users can attach files (images/docs) via a paperclip icon in the chat.
Files are uploaded to Google Drive via the backend script.
Links are returned to the AI context and stored in the Ticket's AttachmentURL column.
Company ID Request Protocol:
Eligibility: AI checks if the user has been employed for 6 months.
Requirements: Must upload Signature and 2x2 Photo.
Data: Collects Name, Position, Project, and Emergency Contact Details.
Disclaimer: AI must warn that the request is subject to HR approval.
Account Support: Requests for Acumatica/Email support must collect Full Name, Department, and Position.
Ticket Closing: The AI has a tool (closeTicket) to mark tickets as "Closed" in the sheet.
4. Backend Configuration (Critical)
The backend is a Google Apps Script acting as an API.
Script URL: (Updated in store.ts)
https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbyK33hOecOAcnljL0XCvyELQPzZC7MRwltKjO_k0f93iVaUGNIdSOrmIP_BhiGj84a3gQ/exec
Google Drive Folder ID: 1LzRc9AXeWAwu4rONAO67bVe7mPxmrbnO
⚠️ Deployment Requirements (Manual Fixes):
To make File Uploads work, the Google Apps Script Manifest (appsscript.json) must be manually updated in the GAS Editor to include these scopes:
code
JSON
"oauthScopes": [
  "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets",
  "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive",
  "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/script.external_request"
]
After updating the manifest, the checkPermissions function must be run once manually to grant access.
5. Security & Deployment
API Key Protection: The hardcoded API Key was removed from deploy.txt due to a leak.
New Workflow:
An .env file must exist on the AWS Server.
Content: API_KEY=your_new_google_ai_key.
The deploy.txt script has been updated to read this file during the build process.
6. Current File Structure
src/App.tsx: Layout container (Full screen chat + conditional TicketManager).
src/store.ts: Manages State, API calls to Google Sheets, and File Upload logic.
src/services/geminiService.ts: Contains the System Instructions, Tools (create/search/close/upload), and Gemini client.
src/components/AIChat.tsx: Chat interface with File Upload button and history management.
src/components/TicketManager.tsx: The floating overlay component.

You need to create a new version of the deployment because **Google Apps Script Web Apps operate on a "Snapshot" system.**

Here is the breakdown:

1.  **Editor vs. Live App**: When you modify code in the Apps Script Editor and hit "Save", you are only changing the **Development** version. The **Live Web App** that your users (and your React app) interact with is locked to a specific **Version Number** (Snapshot) that you previously deployed.
2.  **The Fix Won't Apply**: Even though you pasted the fixed code (`forceAuth`, upload fallback, etc.) into the editor, the URL is still pointing to the **Old Version** of the code where the bugs exist.
3.  **How to Update Without Changing the URL**:

    *   Go to **Deploy** > **Manage Deployments**.
    *   Click the **Pencil Icon (Edit)** next to your active deployment.
    *   Change **Version** from `Existing version` to **`New version`**.
    *   Click **Deploy**.

By doing this, you push your latest code to the *existing* URL, and you don't need to update `store.ts` again. If you choose "New Deployment" instead, Google generates a fresh URL, breaking the connection until you update your React app.

Yes, absolutely. The **Paid Tier (Pay-as-you-go)** has significantly higher rate limits than the Free Tier.

The error you encountered (`429 Resource Exhausted`) usually happens because of the **RPM (Requests Per Minute)** limit on the free tier, not necessarily the total amount of data.

Here is the comparison for the model you are using (**Gemini 1.5/2.5 Flash**):

### 1. The Limits Comparison

| Feature | **Free Tier** (What you are on) | **Paid Tier** (Pay-as-you-go) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Rate Limit (RPM)** | **15 requests per minute** | **2,000 requests per minute** |
| **Daily Limit (RPD)** | **1,500 requests per day** | **Unlimited** (Pay per use) |
| **Tokens Per Minute** | 1 Million TPM | 4 Million+ TPM |
| **Data Privacy** | Google **MAY** use data to improve models | Google **DOES NOT** use your data |

### 2. Why you hit the error
On the Free Tier, if you (or your users) send messages too fast—specifically more than **1 every 4 seconds**—you will hit the **15 RPM** limit and get locked out for a minute. This is very easy to hit in a chat application.

### 3. Is it expensive?
**No.** The "Flash" models are extremely cheap.
For a Helpdesk app like yours, the cost is negligible.

*   **Input Cost:** ~$0.075 per 1 Million tokens.
*   **Output Cost:** ~$0.30 per 1 Million tokens.

**Real-world math:**
You could process **thousands** of support tickets per month, and your bill would likely be **less than $5.00 USD**.

### 4. Recommendation
Since this is a business application (**Ulticon Builders, Inc.**), I strongly recommend switching to the Paid Tier for two reasons:

1.  **Reliability:** You won't hit the "Server Busy" / Quota error anymore.
2.  **Privacy:** Your internal company data (employee names, PIDs, troubleshooting logs) will remain private and won't be used to train Google's models.

**How to Upgrade:**
1.  Go to [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/).
2.  Click on **Settings** or the Project name.
3.  Set up **Billing** (Link a Google Cloud Platform project with a credit card).
4.  Your existing API Key will automatically switch to the Paid limits. No code changes are needed (other than removing the strict limits if you want to).

You **cannot** simply paste this React app code into a WordPress page editor. React apps require a build process and specific server handling.

Standard WordPress installations live in `/var/www/html`. If you use the current `deploy.txt`, **you will overwrite/delete your WordPress site.**

**The Best Approach:**

3.  **Embed (Optional)**: If you really want it inside WordPress, you create a page in WordPress and add an `<iframe src="https://support.yourdomain.com"></iframe>`.
